title: catunaregam stenocarpa
intro: Catunaregam stenocarpa, also known as the "Wild Jasmine", is a plant species native to Southeast Asia. It is a medicinal plant used for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of Catunaregam stenocarpa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various respiratory infections. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create topical creams and ointments for skin conditions and wounds.
